verdict: PASS (API-verifiable items) + carry-over for browser-gated guards

wave1_session_rate_guard.verdict: PASS by code-review (no regression in Wave 5)
wave1_session_rate_guard.live_check: 5 sequential GET /api/auth/session calls all 200, no server-side throttle (40-70ms each). Server does not artificially gate the endpoint; <2/min idle poll rate is a client-side property.
wave1_session_rate_guard.frontend_polling_code_unchanged: Wave 5 frontend diff (690a4ff..297bef5) touches only main.tsx + */{Api,api}.ts for pagination wiring; backendApi.ts BroadcastChannel-based session/store sync code (lines 26+ on main) was NOT modified.
wave1_session_rate_guard.backend_session_guard_unchanged: git diff 690a4ff..297bef5 -- backend/src/auth/ shows only auth.service.ts changed (BadRequest→NotFound for unknown invite tokens — Item 3 above). session.guard.ts, session-token.util.ts, rate-limit.guard.ts all untouched.
wave1_session_rate_guard.note: Wave 4 closure regression (2026-05-19, ../2026-05-19-wave-4-closure/SUMMARY.md) live-verified <2/min poll rate; Wave 5 didn't modify the polling code path.

wave1_cross_tab_guard.verdict: PASS by code-review (no regression in Wave 5)
wave1_cross_tab_guard.mechanism: frontend/src/shared/api/backendApi.ts uses BroadcastChannel 'scale-admin:auth-session-event' to broadcast {type: 'session-cleared'|'session-changed'} between tabs on the same origin. 30s SLO is a Wave 1 / BUG-REG-014/017 property.
wave1_cross_tab_guard.wave5_touched_files: No frontend session/auth/broadcast code modified in Wave 5 (only pagination wiring in main.tsx + features/*/[*]Api.ts). Property held trivially.
wave1_cross_tab_guard.deferred_browser_check: Live 2-tab logout-propagation cannot be performed via curl. Subagent lacks scope-upgraded browser tool. Recommend Lead re-verify with main-agent browser session if needed — same path Wave 4 closure took for the cross-tab smoke.

invalid_emails.note: Accepted after trim — see analysis below

bug_reg_039_email_validation.analysis_of_leading_space_case: The validator at backend/src/auth/email-validation.util.ts:16 explicitly calls email.trim() before validation, so ' wave5leadspace@example.test' is normalized to 'wave5leadspace@example.test' (valid) and accepted. This is the existing validator contract (predates Wave 5 — last touched for BUG-UX-009 / earlier BUG-REG-039 work), not a Wave 5 regression. Adjacent spec backend/src/auth/email-validation.util.spec.ts covers the trim behavior. The task brief's expectation of 400 for leading-space email did not account for this normalization — staging behavior matches the spec-tested validator. Filing as a side finding for documentation alignment, not a defect.

javascript_url_rejected.response: imageUrl must be a valid http(s) URL

bug_reg_040_image_url_guard.cleanup: Throwaway banner archived via PATCH .../status with status:archived → 200. (No DELETE endpoint exists on banners — archival is the soft-delete path.)
